purpose: Machine-readable source-of-truth map for the hosted project documentation. Generated pages should summarize persistent repo artifacts and analysis outputs rather than carrying current release facts only as hand-edited prose.
rules: Do not edit generated HTML to change project state, metrics, release posture, or conformance claims.
Current scores, cost, confidence, release labels, application status, and next priorities must come from committed JSON, Markdown, or reference-artifact inputs.
Templates may contain stable explanatory framing and layout, but each current/readout section must list the artifact family that owns the facts.
Release preflight must fail if this provenance manifest is missing, if declared source artifacts are missing, or if the public/project pages stop rendering provenance.

surfaces.title: Public Project Page
surfaces.summary: Narrative public overview of the release lane, conformance program, resource economics, ingestion, and game catalog.

sections.label: Release identity and current focus
sections.currentness: Build tokens and release dashboard fields are injected by npm run build for the active lane.
sections.riskIfStale: The public page can show a stale version/date/focus even when the game artifact has moved.

sections.label: Conformance readout and release gates
sections.currentness: Score bars and gate cards are generated from the release conformance dashboard artifact.
sections.riskIfStale: Scores may become narrative claims rather than measured dashboard outputs.

sections.label: Value versus compute
sections.currentness: Resource cards, investment rows, and the self-critical work-block read are generated from economics fields, retrospective artifacts, and linked economics docs.
sections.riskIfStale: CPU/GPU-equivalent spend can drift away from the measured conformance gains it is supposed to justify.

sections.label: Reference ingestion
sections.currentness: Ingestion cards are generated from dashboard ingestion rows and linked to maintained ingestion/reference docs.
sections.riskIfStale: The public story can imply repeatable ingestion without showing the artifacts that make it repeatable.

sections.label: Game conformance catalog
sections.currentness: Game cards come from dashboard game profiles; fuller alien/audio/stage/persona tables come from the application and catalog artifacts.
sections.riskIfStale: Game-specific conformance can collapse into a generic platform summary.

sections.label: Architecture and code review learning
sections.currentness: Summary cards, issue-note rows, and learning patterns are generated from the review-learning ledger artifact.
sections.riskIfStale: Architecture and code-review lessons can become hidden repo artifacts instead of visible release-learning evidence.

surfaces.title: Hosted Project Guide
surfaces.summary: Long-form hosted guide that renders maintained project sections plus source Markdown documents.

sections.label: Guide overview and source document index
sections.currentness: Guide cards and source-doc sections are generated from project-guide.json and listed source Markdown files, including the Guardians stage assessment, the hosted opening-slice baseline, and the deeper Guardians conformance plans.
sections.riskIfStale: The hosted guide can stop matching the maintained repo documentation and push readers back into raw source browsing for core project understanding.

sections.label: Documentation provenance
sections.currentness: The hosted guide renders this manifest as a build-time table.
sections.riskIfStale: Readers and release reviewers lose the map from public statements back to durable artifacts.

surfaces.title: Hosted White Paper
surfaces.summary: Hosted narrative surface for the living white paper, its citation ledger, and the supporting white-paper project area.

sections.label: White-paper overview and project-area framing
sections.currentness: The hosted page renders its guide framing from white-paper.json and the current narrative/citation sources from committed Markdown files.
sections.riskIfStale: The public narrative can drift away from the actual maintained white-paper project area or its current draft.

sections.label: White-paper release memory
sections.currentness: The hosted page links directly to the versioned white-paper release record so the narrative can be compared over time instead of being overwritten without memory.
sections.riskIfStale: The current paper can imply historical continuity without showing how earlier drafts were preserved.

surfaces.title: Hosted Release Notes
surfaces.summary: Hosted readable release-note archive generated from structured release notes plus committed detailed release-note source documents when available.

sections.label: Release-note index and latest release framing
sections.currentness: The note index and current release metadata are generated from the active lane build plus release-notes.json.
sections.riskIfStale: The dashboard can point to a notes page that no longer matches the current lane or note ordering.

sections.label: Detailed release-note documents
sections.currentness: When a note declares sourceDoc, the hosted page renders that Markdown directly during build.
sections.riskIfStale: Readers may only see thin summary notes even when a fuller committed release narrative exists.

surfaces.title: Aurora Application Guide
surfaces.summary: Aurora-owned audio, visual, alien, stage, target-artifact, artifact-conformance, and persona catalog.

sections.label: Audio and visual catalog
sections.currentness: Guide rows are generated from application-guide.json and freshness-checked against runtime audio cue durations.
sections.riskIfStale: Audio review buttons and event descriptions can drift away from runtime cues.

sections.label: Artifact conformance status
sections.currentness: The artifact scorecard, target artifact coverage, and persona performance distribution are generated from analysis outputs rather than single static claims.
sections.riskIfStale: Sprite/audio/visual/target-artifact/persona claims can look precise while no longer matching the latest harness artifacts.

surfaces.title: Conformance Dashboard
surfaces.summary: Primary live score, confidence, cost, ingestion, and investment dashboard.

sections.label: Dashboard data and metrics
sections.currentness: The HTML and raw JSON are built from the same release-conformance artifact.
sections.riskIfStale: The dashboard can show polished charts over stale or missing metric data.
